ai.issueType: CustomButton div element not keyboard accessible
ai.description: The CustomButton component renders a div with an onClick handler but no role="button", tabindex, or keyboard event handlers. This makes it inaccessible to keyboard users and screen readers, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

ai.issueType: Navigation toggle button missing aria-expanded state
ai.description: The Menu button toggles the visibility of the navigation menu but lacks aria-expanded to indicate the current state (expanded/collapsed). This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is currently open or closed.

ai.issueType: Navigation toggle missing aria-controls attribute
ai.description: The Menu button controls the visibility of the ul menu but lacks aria-controls to programmatically associate it with the controlled element.

ai.issueType: Navigation menu missing id for aria-controls reference
ai.description: The ul element serves as the controlled region for the Menu button but lacks an id attribute, preventing aria-controls from functioning correctly.

ai.issueType: Tab button missing id attribute for aria-labelledby reference
ai.description: The tab button uses key={tab.id} but lacks an id attribute. The associated tabpanel at line 58 references aria-labelledby={`tab-${tab.id}`}, which targets a non-existent ID, breaking the programmatic relationship for screen readers.

ai.issueType: Tab button missing aria-controls attribute
ai.description: The tab button controls the visibility of its associated tabpanel but lacks aria-controls to establish the programmatic relationship required by the tabs pattern.

ai.issueType: Tabpanel missing id for aria-controls reference
ai.description: The tabpanel element lacks an id attribute, preventing the tab button's aria-controls from referencing it correctly.

ai.issueType: Form textarea missing aria-invalid for validation errors
ai.description: The textarea element displays validation errors but lacks aria-invalid to communicate the invalid state to screen readers when errors[field.name] exists.

ai.issueType: Form input missing aria-invalid for validation errors
ai.description: The input element displays validation errors but lacks aria-invalid to communicate the invalid state to screen readers when errors[field.name] exists.

ai.issueType: Form error message not associated with input via aria-describedby
ai.description: The error message span is not programmatically associated with its corresponding input field. Screen readers cannot announce the error message when the field is focused.

ai.issueType: Form email input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The email input (type="email") collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and violating WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.

ai.issueType: Form name input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The name input (name="name") collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="name" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.

ai.issueType: Form phone input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The telephone input (type="tel") collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="tel" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.

ai.issueType: Modal Escape key handler not implemented
ai.description: The handleEscape function detects the Escape key but does not call onClose(), preventing keyboard users from closing the modal using the Escape key.

ai.issueType: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
ai.description: The dialog element lacks aria-modal="true", which prevents screen readers from correctly handling the modal context and restricting navigation to the modal content.

ai.issueType: Modal missing initial focus management
ai.description: When the modal opens, focus is not moved to the modal dialog or its close button, causing keyboard users to lose their place in the document. The modalRef is not focused programmatically.

ai.issueType: Dropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded state
ai.description: The dropdown toggle button controls the visibility of the listbox but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the dropdown is currently open or closed.

ai.issueType: Dropdown toggle missing aria-controls attribute
ai.description: The dropdown toggle button lacks aria-controls to programmatically associate it with the listbox element it controls.

ai.issueType: Dropdown toggle button missing id for label association
ai.description: The dropdown button lacks an id attribute, preventing the label element from associating with it via htmlFor.

ai.issueType: Dropdown label not programmatically associated with button
ai.description: The label element renders text but lacks htmlFor attribute to associate with the dropdown button, and the button lacks a corresponding id.

ai.issueType: Dropdown listbox missing id for aria-controls reference
ai.description: The ul element acting as listbox lacks an id attribute, preventing aria-controls on the toggle button from referencing it.

ai.issueType: Dropdown option elements not keyboard accessible
ai.description: The li elements with role="option" have onClick handlers but are not focusable (no tabindex) and have no keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space). This prevents keyboard users from selecting options.

ai.issueType: Cart toggle button missing aria-expanded state
ai.description: The View Cart button toggles the visibility of the cart modal but lacks aria-expanded to indicate the modal's current state.

ai.issueType: Cart toggle button missing aria-controls attribute
ai.description: The View Cart button controls the cart modal but lacks aria-controls to programmatically associate it with the modal dialog.

ai.issueType: LoadingSpinner animation lacks reduced motion support
ai.description: The spinner uses CSS animation 'spin 1s linear infinite' without checking for prefers-reduced-motion, which can trigger vestibular disorders in users sensitive to motion.

ai.issueType: Cart item count updates not announced to screen readers
ai.description: The cart count ({cartItems.length}) updates dynamically when items are added, but there is no aria-live region to announce these changes to screen reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

ai.issueType: Tab panel content changes not announced to screen readers
ai.description: When users click a tab to switch panels, the content changes visibly but no aria-live region announces the new panel content or the selection change to screen readers.

ai.issueType: Dropdown selection changes not announced to screen readers
ai.description: When an option is selected in the dropdown, the button text updates to show the selected value, but this change is not announced to screen readers because there is no aria-live region associated with the selection.

ai.issueType: Interactive div missing role and keyboard support
ai.description: The CustomButton component uses a <div> with an onClick handler, which is not natively focusable or clickable via keyboard. Screen reader users and keyboard-only users cannot access this control.

ai.issueType: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
ai.description: The <nav> element lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions, which is required when multiple navs exist or for best practice clarity.

ai.issueType: Tab panel aria-labelledby references non-existent ID
ai.description: The panel's aria-labelledby points to tab-${tab.id}, but the tab button does not have this ID, resulting in a broken ARIA reference.

ai.issueType: Tab buttons missing keyboard navigation
ai.description: Tabs require arrow key navigation to move focus between tabs, which is not implemented, hindering keyboard users.

ai.issueType: Modal focus not managed on open
ai.description: When the modal opens, focus is not moved inside the dialog, trapping keyboard users on the underlying page.

ai.issueType: Loading spinner missing accessible role and label
ai.description: The animated div conveys status but has no role or label, so screen reader users are unaware of the loading state.

eslintJsxA11y.description: Avoid non-native interactive elements. If using native HTML is not possible, add an appropriate role and support for tabbing, mouse, keyboard, and touch inputs to an interactive content element.
Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Elements with the ARIA role "option" must have the following attributes defined: aria-selected

webhint.description: Elements must meet enhanced color contrast ratio thresholds: Element has insufficient color contrast of 6.65 (foreground color: #0000ff, background color: #e0e0ff, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 7:1
